title: brain db restore <backupRef> [--export-challenge | --authorization <path>] — --json success output
description: Privileged emergency restore of a verified prior ledger backup per ledger-backup-contract.md §restore. Authorized ONLY by an OS-presence assertion bound to the broker challenge (op db.restore) or the non-interactive --export-challenge → sign → --authorization flow (security-broker-contract.md §7); --yes never authorizes. On authorization: acquires exclusive vault-maintenance + ledger-maintenance locks, verifies the bundle (decrypt, content hash, schema), transactionally restores the ledger tables, establishes a fresh backup_watermark at the restored cut, then runs the registered post-restore rebuild hooks (projection rebuild; index rebuild once Phase 3 registers it). Emits a db.restore ledger audit row (D6). Without an authorization, exits 6 (action-required) and, with --export-challenge, emits an AuthorizationChallenge (see security-broker-contract.md §7.1) instead of this success object.

restoredFromRef.description: The backup ref that was restored.

restoredSeq.description: The audit seq the restored ledger is now at (the fresh watermark).

priorSeq.description: The audit seq the ledger was at before restore (the accepted loss window is priorSeq − restoredSeq when positive).

rebuildHooksRun.description: The post-restore rebuild hooks executed, in order (e.g. projection-rebuild, index-rebuild).

x-atlas-contract.summary: Broker-authorized destructive restore of a verified backup, then post-restore rebuild hooks.

flags.description: Emit the broker AuthorizationChallenge (op db.restore, bound to backupRef + content hash) to stdout and exit 6 instead of restoring; sign it out of band with an enrolled approver key.
Path to a signed AuthorizationResponse produced from a prior --export-challenge; presents it to the broker to authorize the restore.

sideEffects: transactionally replaces the ledger tables from the backup
establishes a fresh backup_watermark at the restored cut
runs registered post-restore rebuild hooks (projection; index in Phase 3)
writes a db.restore ledger audit row (D6)
prohibitedEffects: no vault content mutation (only ledger + derived projections)
never authorized by --yes; never bypasses the broker challenge
never runs without both vault-maintenance and ledger-maintenance exclusive locks

errorCodes.when: unknown flag/argument, missing <backupRef>, or --export-challenge combined with --authorization
the backup fails decryptability or content-hash verification
the backup's stamped schema version is incompatible with the current binary
the AuthorizationResponse fails broker verification (bad signature, expired/replayed nonce, wrong signer, drift)
the transactional restore or a post-restore hook failed (prior DB left intact)
